questions.question: Use a correlation matrix to explore the relationships between the variables Age, Income, Education, and Occupation. Identify the variables that are positively correlated with each other and those that are negatively correlated. (4 marks)

A pair plot is used to visualize the relationships between the variables Age, Sex, and Smoking. Identify the quadrant(s) where the points fall that suggest a strong negative correlation between Age and Smoking. (5 marks)

Use a correlation matrix to identify the variable that is most strongly positively correlated with Housing Price. List the top 2 variables that are positively correlated with Housing Price and explain the possible reasons for this relationship. (6 marks)
